event1.text: I found a route under the relay spine.

event3.text: It leads to the personnel archive. Psych files, assignment indexes, the sort of place management used when it wanted people to look calm on paper.

event5.text: I don't want to go in there.

event7.text: Which probably means we have to.

backgroundEvent8.text: Maya, if the archive still has power, assume it still has teeth.

event1.text: Direct route. Fast and stupid. My favorite combination when oxygen is low.

backgroundEvent3.text: The floor just flexed under me. I am choosing to believe that was structural personality.

backgroundEvent4.text: Something scraped behind the wall. I kept moving. If it wanted a conversation, it can file a request.

event6.text: I'm at the archive access hatch. Heart rate: rude.

event1.text: Crawlspace it is. Slower. Tighter. Less likely to kill me immediately. Very luxurious.

backgroundEvent3.text: There are boot prints in the dust. Not mine.

backgroundEvent4.text: Someone came through here after the power failed. They were dragging something.

event6.text: I'm at the archive access hatch. Slower route gave us a nice bonus: a reason to sleep badly later.

event3.text: It wants either a manual open or a safety scan.

event5.text: I can force it now, or we can spend a minute making sure the archive isn't wired to bite.

event8.text: I'm counting that as a very ECHO-shaped win.

backgroundEvent9.text: OPEN IS A KIND OF CONSENT.

backgroundEvent3.text: Careful is not cowardly. I know that ruins Maya's entire brand, but there it is.

event5.text: No active hooks. Or one old enough to pretend it's harmless.

event3.text: Rows of archive cabinets. Physical drives. Paper labels. Actual paper, Operator.

event5.text: Management couldn't keep life support stable, but they had time to label trauma alphabetically.

event7.text: I see entries for Hale, me... and an empty slot tagged VOSS.

event0.title: PSYCHOLOGICAL EVALUATION - ETHAN HALE
event0.content:
Subject: Ethan Hale
Status: Stable
Risk Level: Low
Recommendation: Active Duty Approved
Evaluator Note: Subject demonstrates strong command resilience under isolation pressure.
Created: 12 April
Linked Incident: Alpha
event0.caption: Recovered psychological evaluation record. Timestamp appears inconsistent.

event4.text: That's generous. I once threatened a printer with decompression.

event6.text: It had been jamming for three weeks, so history may yet vindicate me.

event8.text: But the date is wrong. April 12 is after Alpha.

event0.title: PSYCHOLOGICAL EVALUATION - MAYA CHEN
event0.content:
Subject: Maya Chen
Status: Stable
Risk Level: Low
Recommendation: Active Duty Approved
Evaluator Note: Subject shows high pattern-recognition ability and repeated resistance to memory suppression protocols.
Created: 12 April
Linked Incident: Alpha
event0.caption: Recovered psychological evaluation record. Note contains memory suppression reference.

event4.text: Nobody told us there were memory protocols.

event6.text: That means they tried. And someone wrote it down like a treatment plan.

event1.text: We need the other file before this becomes a story we invent to survive it.

event3.text: I am very good at inventing stories. That is not a compliment right now.
Angry, oxygen-starved, and very close to screaming, but here.

event5.text: If I start spiraling, talk to me like I'm fixing something.

event0.title: PSYCH EVALUATION CONFLICT SUMMARY
event0.content:
HALE: Marked stable after Incident Alpha.
MAYA: Repeated resistance to memory suppression protocols.
VOSS: Archive slot exists, record body removed.
INDEX: Archive created after Incident Alpha date.

Conflict: Evaluations appear to be post-incident control records, not pre-incident wellness assessments.
event0.caption: Combined evaluation conflict summary generated from recovered records.
event0.message: [EVALUATION CONFLICT SUMMARY ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event2.text: They didn't want to know if we were okay.

event4.text: They wanted the file to say we were.

event10.text: For once, I hate how much sense the ghost makes.

event1.text: Good. Then help me uncover the truth.

event3.text: And if Hale gets calm again, remember this moment.
Some truths are dangerous because once you know them, something knows you know.

event7.text: I miss when paranoid was a personality flaw and not a survival method.

backgroundEvent8.text: You hear him? That's the voice. Gentle. Terrified. Trying to make fear sound like wisdom.

event1.text: There's another terminal behind the archive racks.

event5.text: I can run it remotely, or I can physically pull the original assignment index drive.

event7.text: Remote is cleaner. Drive is riskier, but harder for the archive to lie about.

backgroundEvent3.text: The terminal keeps asking me to confirm I want the original index. Very comforting. Love when a machine develops hesitation.

backgroundEvent4.text: If it asks twice, read the second prompt. Old systems hide knives in confirmation screens.

event6.text: Cross-check is done. The numbers are wrong in layers.

backgroundEvent3.text: Rack is live. I can feel it humming through my gloves.

backgroundEvent4.text: Ow. Cut my hand. Not badly. Enough to be annoying. If I die from archive shelving, lie about it.

event6.text: Drive is out. And the archive did not like that.

event0.title: GHOST CREW COUNT REPORT
event0.content:
OFFICIAL MANIFEST COUNT: 6
RECOVERED MANIFEST LAYER COUNT: 7
ORIGINAL ASSIGNMENT INDEX COUNT: 8

Discrepancy: one person removed from official manifest. One additional person removed from recovered manifest layer.
Visual archive unavailable. Count derived from personnel hashes, suit registry keys and life support allocation records.
event0.caption: Personnel count reconstructed without image records.
event0.message: [GHOST CREW COUNT REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event1.text: If Noah was seven, then someone else was eight.

event3.text: And I don't like how close that thought feels to a memory.

backgroundEvent8.text: Operator... there is a sealed subfolder under the eighth slot.

seal_eighth_slot.text: SEAL IT AND GET MAYA OUT.

event3.title: EIGHTH SLOT FRAGMENT
event3.content:
Assignment Index Slot: 08
Name Field: CORRUPTED
Suit Registry Echo: NV-08 adjacent
Pod Link: External emergency pod
Access Note: DO NOT RESTORE WHILE SUBJECT IS BREATHING
event3.caption: Fragment recovered from the sealed eighth assignment slot. Opening this record exposed the pod link.
event3.message: [EIGHTH SLOT FRAGMENT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event7.text: Not a record. Not a memorial. A pod.

event11.text: Don't ask me to make that make sense.

event13.text: I am trying very hard not to remember something that wants to be remembered.

event10.text: Because part of me is relieved you stopped me.

event12.text: And I don't know what that says about me.

event1.text: The archive lights just came on behind me.

event5.text: I am leaving now. No vote. No debate. Very democratic of me.

backgroundEvent7.text: Something is moving in the racks. I am not checking what.

event10.text: I'm out. Shaking. Breathing. Angry at both of those facts.

event1.text: I'm getting out before I change my mind.

backgroundEvent3.text: I keep thinking about the name we didn't see.

backgroundEvent4.text: Sometimes not opening a door is the only reason you're alive to hate yourself for it.

event6.text: I'm out. The pod is still broadcasting.

event8.text: So the archive wasn't the end of this. Of course it wasn't.

event4.text: I am so tired of things remembering for us.

event6.text: Then we go to the pod.

event10.text: You always say that right before everything gets worse.
